message:
fix(core): collation-aware DISTINCT deduplication + WHERE clause fast-path guard

Two correctness fixes that bring conformance from 920→932 pass:

1. COLLATE NOCASE DISTINCT (fixes 6 tests): SELECT DISTINCT now uses
   column-declared collation for deduplication. Added dedup_rows_collated()
   that compares text values through the CollationRegistry when a non-BINARY
   collation is declared (e.g. NOCASE). Added select_result_collations()
   to extract per-column collation at prepare time. Updated all 15
   dedup_rows call sites (12 execute-statement + 3 prepared-statement).

2. GROUP BY mem-scan WHERE guard (fixes 5 tests): The streaming GROUP BY
   mem-scan fast path now skips when SELECT has a WHERE clause, falling
   through to VDBE which correctly compiles and applies the predicate.

Also: cargo fmt reformatting of V1/V2 test files.

Conformance: 920→932 pass, 18→6 fail.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
